flags.rewound_from_stage0_5_pivot: case 6b + regression pattern (a) — same novelty-floor flag persists across all three attempts; the producer's own note self-disclaims flagship status (lines 213-221, 596-603), and the reviewer's reject is at the conjecture level (the chain-refined source-cover closure is structurally a finite-chain-rule certificate inside an established framework, not an open-regime result). Re-deriving the same .tex would burn budget — the artifact needs a new angle that names a specific published estimator/algorithm to refute or strictly extend, or a separation/equivalence frontier against DoSearch/gID/Lee-Ghassami-Shpitser. Override of stage_0 heuristic: defect is conjecture-level, not derivation-level.
flags.stage_neg1_fallback: stage_neg1 requested but pivot budget exhausted (NEG1_PIVOT_BUDGET=3): Case 6b — Stage 0.5 reject is conjecture-level (novelty tier floor): the artifact itself disclaims flagship status and proves only a chain-rule closure under assumed singleton source invariances, which under the flagship floor directive cannot be salvaged by re-deriving the same conjecture; pivot to a new angle.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp identifiability of an interventional causal effect from a heterogeneous collection of k partially-overlapping observational distributions under a selection-diagram constraint set: a closed-form iff identifiability characterization extending Bareinboim-Pearl (2014, 2016) data fusion from two sources to k sources, with a polynomial-time decision algorithm in the size of the diagram and an explicit counterexample witness construction certifying non-identifiability when the iff fails, recovering Bareinboim-Pearl as the k=2 special case
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue CausalSmith prior art remains adjacent but not duplicative: eid_proximal_phase concerns nonunique proximal bridges, eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator concerns mediator/proxy partial-ID polytopes, and pid_long_term_surrogate concerns long-term surrogate bounds. None states a k-source exact-ID source-cover criterion or an overlap-hedge witness preserving selected observational source laws.","published_axis":"Closest published comparators are LeeGhassamiShpitser2024DataFusionSelection for general systematic-selection data-fusion algorithms, TikkaHyttinenKarvanen2021DoSearch for arbitrary-input do-search, LeeCorreaBareinboim2020GID and KivvaMokhtarianEtesamiKiyavash2022RevisitGID for thicket/hedgelet-style gID witnesses, and LeeBareinboim2020PartialObservability for partial measured-variable inputs. The flagship epsilon-gap is the restricted observational factor-local source-cover branch with a canonical witness preserving all k selected source margins and typed invariance clauses; the GID-PO NP-completeness comparison is now stated as conditional on its completeness conjecture."}
seed_list: Prior work BareinboimPearl2016DataFusion would imply that heterogeneous data fusion can be organized by source, regime, and sampling formulas. Our seed conjectures that in the pure observational partial-overlap regime, identification is governed by a source-cover certificate whose failure creates an overlap hedge preserving every source margin.

Prior work BareinboimPearl2014MZTransport establishes multiple-environment transportability with limited experiments. Our seed conjectures that removing all source experiments creates a new observational obstruction: pairwise-covered c-factors can still fail because no single source preserves the joint district boundary needed by the target effect.

Prior work TikkaHyttinenKarvanen2021DoSearch would suggest arbitrary incomplete-source inputs are best handled by symbolic do-calculus search. Our seed conjectures that the factor-local observational selection-diagram subclass has a closed-form polynomial source-cover iff criterion plus a canonical witness when search would fail.

Prior work ShpitserPearl2006ID establishes hedge witnesses for ordinary ID failure. Our seed conjectures that ordinary hedges are not source-compatible in k-source observational fusion because the witness must keep all selected overlapping source laws fixed while changing the target intervention.

Prior work BareinboimPearl2012TransportCompleteness establishes transportability obstructions for selection diagrams. Our seed conjectures that a multisource observational obstruction must additionally encode variable coverage and cross-source kernel equalities, producing a stricter overlap hedge than the published transport hedge.

Prior work BareinboimTianPearl2014Selection establishes recoverability from selection-biased data plus some external unbiased information. Our seed conjectures that multiple biased but overlapping observational sources can recover an effect with no unbiased source exactly when each required district factor is source-coverable.

Prior work BareinboimTian2015RecoveringEffects characterizes selected-effect recoverability in Markovian models and gives semi-Markovian sufficient conditions. Our seed conjectures that an overlap-ancestral semi-Markovian k-source fragment regains completeness through a factor-local source-cover condition.

Prior work TikkaHyttinenKarvanen2021DoSearch flags combined transportability and selection bias with multiple selection sources as challenging. Our seed conjectures that simultaneous transport and source-specific selection has a polynomial certificate or multisource selection-transport obstruction in the observational factor-local class.

Prior work CrumpHotzImbensMitnik2006Overlap would imply that limited support is an estimation-target problem once a formula is known. Our seed conjectures that k-source graphical fusion needs minimal source-specific positivity annotations, because a graph-cover formula can become nonidentification when its denominator is positive only in the wrong source.

Prior work LannersRudinVolfovskyParikh2025DataFusionPI studies partial-identification data fusion under exchangeability failures. Our seed conjectures that when k-source selection-diagram point ID fails, the same overlap hedge can seed sharp finite-dimensional bounds over SCMs matching all selected source laws.

Prior work LiLuedtke2023EfficientFusion establishes efficiency gains from aligned source fusion. Our seed conjectures that under bridge failure or bounded source drift, the alignment equations define a nonrectangular latent-completion polytope whose sharp endpoints are not obtained by separately optimizing each source.

Prior proposal pid_long_term_surrogate_v1 would predict that terminal-only transport endpoints can be tightened by panel surrogate increments. Our seed conjectures that a two-source selected observational overlap witness can remove one terminal copula face while preserving all observed increments.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BareinboimPearl2016DataFusion would imply that heterogeneous data fusion can be organized by source, regime, and sampling formulas. Our seed conjectures that in the pure observational partial-overlap regime, identification is governed by a source-cover certificate whose failure creates an overlap hedge preserving every source margin.

anchor_paper.author: Elias Bareinboim and Judea Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: Bareinboim and Pearl frame data fusion as combining heterogeneous populations, regimes, and sampling processes, but not as a k-source observational iff criterion with witness construction.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends two-source/multiple-source data fusion pattern, not blocked because the proposed novelty is the overlap-hedge obstruction preserving all source margins, absent from the published regime/external-data formulas.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BareinboimPearl2016DataFusion because that paper already names heterogeneous data fusion.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the paper frames the program and examples, but does not give a closed-form observational k-source iff criterion or a multisource witness preserving partially overlapping selected laws.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BareinboimPearl2014MZTransport establishes multiple-environment transportability with limited experiments. Our seed conjectures that removing all source experiments creates a new observational obstruction: pairwise-covered c-factors can still fail because no single source preserves the joint district boundary needed by the target effect.

anchor_paper.author: Elias Bareinboim and Judea Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: Bareinboim and Pearl prove completeness for transportability from multiple environments with limited experiments, a different regime from purely observational partially overlapping source laws.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends experimental transportability to observational sources pattern, not blocked because the mechanism is a failure mode: source experiments removed, partial overlap forces a new district-boundary obstruction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from mz-transportability completeness because observational laws are just a special case with no experiments.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the available inputs are partially observed selected source laws, so the witness must preserve source margins and overlap constraints that mz-transportability obstructions do not encode.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work TikkaHyttinenKarvanen2021DoSearch would suggest arbitrary incomplete-source inputs are best handled by symbolic do-calculus search. Our seed conjectures that the factor-local observational selection-diagram subclass has a closed-form polynomial source-cover iff criterion plus a canonical witness when search would fail.

anchor_paper.author: Santtu Tikka, Antti Hyttinen, and Juha Karvanen
anchor_paper.claim: Tikka, Hyttinen, and Karvanen give a sound do-calculus search over arbitrary observational and experimental source distributions, with completeness only for classes already characterized by do-calculus.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Method-extension pattern, not blocked because the proposal replaces search traces with a theorem-level iff and nonidentification witness for a named observational subclass.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from do-search because it already searches arbitrary observational and experimental source distributions.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: do-search is an algorithmic symbolic search tool; it does not state the closed-form source-cover criterion or overlap-hedge witness for this restricted class.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ShpitserPearl2006ID establishes hedge witnesses for ordinary ID failure. Our seed conjectures that ordinary hedges are not source-compatible in k-source observational fusion because the witness must keep all selected overlapping source laws fixed while changing the target intervention.

anchor_paper.author: Ilya Shpitser and Judea Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: Shpitser and Pearl give hedge-style completeness witnesses for ordinary interventional identification, but not for multiple overlapping selected source laws.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/witness pattern, not blocked because it refines a named published hedge theorem and promotes the obstruction to an open class of source-compatible witnesses, not a hand-tuned degenerate example.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the ordinary hedge construction because any non-ID problem has a hedge.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the ordinary hedge need only preserve one observational law, whereas k-source fusion requires preserving several selected overlapping laws and invariance clauses simultaneously.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BareinboimPearl2012TransportCompleteness establishes transportability obstructions for selection diagrams. Our seed conjectures that a multisource observational obstruction must additionally encode variable coverage and cross-source kernel equalities, producing a stricter overlap hedge than the published transport hedge.

anchor_paper.author: Elias Bareinboim and Judea Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: Bareinboim and Pearl establish completeness for transportability, but the obstruction is not stated as a k-source observational witness preserving all partially overlapping source distributions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/witness pattern, not blocked because the refuted shortcut is the named transportability obstruction used outside its input regime and the proposed witness preserves nondegenerate observed source laws.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from transportability completeness because selection diagrams already encode source differences.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: transportability completeness does not require witnesses to match k partially overlapping selected observational distributions with source-specific measured-variable sets.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BareinboimTianPearl2014Selection establishes recoverability from selection-biased data plus some external unbiased information. Our seed conjectures that multiple biased but overlapping observational sources can recover an effect with no unbiased source exactly when each required district factor is source-coverable.

anchor_paper.author: Elias Bareinboim, Jin Tian, and Judea Pearl
anchor_paper.claim: Bareinboim, Tian, and Pearl give complete graphical and algorithmic conditions for recovering conditional probabilities from selection-biased data, plus partial-source unbiased-data conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption-variation pattern, not blocked because it predicts a regime change: no unbiased source is available, yet multiple biased overlaps can jointly recover exactly the missing district factors.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from selection recoverability with external data because overlapping sources are just external data.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the external-data cases do not characterize arbitrary k selected observational overlaps or give a target-effect witness when the overlaps fail.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BareinboimTian2015RecoveringEffects characterizes selected-effect recoverability in Markovian models and gives semi-Markovian sufficient conditions. Our seed conjectures that an overlap-ancestral semi-Markovian k-source fragment regains completeness through a factor-local source-cover condition.

anchor_paper.author: Elias Bareinboim and Jin Tian
anchor_paper.claim: Bareinboim and Tian completely characterize recoverable effects in Markovian models with simultaneous confounding and selection, and give sufficient conditions for semi-Markovian models.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens/extends assumption pattern, not blocked because the proposed overlap-ancestral class aims to recover completeness in a semi-Markovian multisource regime where the published result gives only sufficient conditions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BareinboimTian2015 because semi-Markovian sufficient conditions can be applied source by source.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: source-by-source recoverability does not decide whether separately recovered pieces can be fused across partial overlaps to identify one target intervention.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work TikkaHyttinenKarvanen2021DoSearch flags combined transportability and selection bias with multiple selection sources as challenging. Our seed conjectures that simultaneous transport and source-specific selection has a polynomial certificate or multisource selection-transport obstruction in the observational factor-local class.

anchor_paper.author: Santtu Tikka, Antti Hyttinen, and Juha Karvanen
anchor_paper.claim: Tikka, Hyttinen, and Karvanen explicitly list combined transportability and selection bias and multiple sources of selection bias as challenging extensions not previously considered in the literature.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension pattern, not blocked because the proposed mechanism is a phase boundary between neutralizable discrepancies and genuine target-effect ambiguity, with a witness on failure.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from do-search because combined selection and transportability are within do-calculus expressivity.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: expressivity and search soundness do not supply a polynomial closed-form criterion or a canonical failure certificate for the named factor-local observational subclass.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CrumpHotzImbensMitnik2006Overlap would imply that limited support is an estimation-target problem once a formula is known. Our seed conjectures that k-source graphical fusion needs minimal source-specific positivity annotations, because a graph-cover formula can become nonidentification when its denominator is positive only in the wrong source.

anchor_paper.author: Richard K. Crump, V. Joseph Hotz, Guido W. Imbens, and Oscar A. Mitnik
anchor_paper.claim: Crump, Hotz, Imbens, and Mitnik address limited overlap by changing the estimand, highlighting support as a first-order obstacle outside purely graphical criteria.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Support-regime pattern, not blocked because it predicts a formula-validity failure specific to source-indexed denominators, not a generic overlap warning.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from standard positivity conditions because every causal formula needs denominators.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the k-source setting requires deciding which source must supply each denominator, and positivity in another source does not validate the fusion functional.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LannersRudinVolfovskyParikh2025DataFusionPI studies partial-identification data fusion under exchangeability failures. Our seed conjectures that when k-source selection-diagram point ID fails, the same overlap hedge can seed sharp finite-dimensional bounds over SCMs matching all selected source laws.

anchor_paper.author: Quinn Lanners, Cynthia Rudin, Alexander Volfovsky, and Harsh Parikh
anchor_paper.claim: Lanners, Rudin, Volfovsky, and Parikh propose partial-identification data fusion when no-unobserved-confounding and cross-source exchangeability can fail together, but not arbitrary selection-diagram k-source bounds.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Partial-ID fallback pattern, not blocked because the mechanism is a graph-specific sharp feasible-set construction after exact-ID failure, not a generic bounds application.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from partial-identification data fusion because bounds are always optimization over compatible laws.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the cited work uses exchangeability/confounding structures, not arbitrary selected observational source margins constrained by a selection diagram.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LiLuedtke2023EfficientFusion establishes efficiency gains from aligned source fusion. Our seed conjectures that under bridge failure or bounded source drift, the alignment equations define a nonrectangular latent-completion polytope whose sharp endpoints are not obtained by separately optimizing each source.

anchor_paper.author: Sijia Li and Alex Luedtke
anchor_paper.claim: Li and Luedtke characterize efficiency gains from fusing multiple aligned data sources for smooth parameters, leaving sharp identified sets under bridge failure or bounded drift outside the point-ID efficiency setup.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Partial-ID extension pattern, not blocked because the proposed mechanism is nonrectangular cross-source compatibility under bridge failure, not a routine efficiency-to-bounds translation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from finite-dimensional LP sharpness because latent completions are always polytopes.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the unresolved point is the exact cross-source compatibility and attainability proof under bridge, measurement, and drift restrictions, not LP existence alone.

seed_details.one_line: Prior proposal pid_long_term_surrogate_v1 would predict that terminal-only transport endpoints can be tightened by panel surrogate increments. Our seed conjectures that a two-source selected observational overlap witness can remove one terminal copula face while preserving all observed increments.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: strengthening_paths[2]: Add a binary numerical boundary example showing one terminal-only endpoint face removed.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample pattern, not blocked because the target is a named prior-proposal reviewer gap requiring an observable boundary witness, not a self-invented shortcut.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the rejected pid_long_term_surrogate proposal restated.
seed_details.rebuttal: False as a seed for exact-ID only if re-anchored to selected-source overlap witnesses; otherwise it remains a partial-ID surrogate-panel angle and was not chosen.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Bareinboim and Pearl (2016, PNAS, https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.1510507113) frames data fusion as combining heterogeneous populations, regimes, and sampling processes.
Pearl and Bareinboim (2014, Statistical Science) and Bareinboim and Pearl (2012, AAAI, https://doi.org/10.1609/aaai.v26i1.8232) establish selection-diagram transportability and complete algorithms for source-target transport.
Bareinboim and Pearl (2014, NeurIPS, https://papers.nips.cc/paper/5536-transportability-from-multiple-environments-with-limited-experiments-completeness-results) gives completeness for mz-transportability, but the setting is built around experimental or limited experimental information, not purely observational k-source partial overlap.
Lee and Honavar (2013, AAAI) studies m-transportability from multiple environments and supplies another multi-environment anchor.
Bareinboim, Tian, and Pearl (2014, AAAI, https://doi.org/10.1609/aaai.v28i1.9074) and Bareinboim and Tian (2015, AAAI, https://doi.org/10.1609/aaai.v29i1.9679) handle selection-bias recoverability, but not arbitrary k-source observational fusion.
Correa, Tian, and Bareinboim (2018, AAAI) gives complete generalized adjustment under selection and confounding, a narrower adjustment fragment.
Shpitser and Pearl (2006, AAAI; 2008, JMLR) and Huang and Valtorta (2006, AAAI) provide ordinary-ID completeness and hedge/counterexample substrate.
Tikka, Hyttinen, and Karvanen (2021, JSS, https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v099.i05) is the closest arbitrary-input tool: do-search handles multiple incomplete observational and experimental distributions, but it is not the desired closed-form polynomial iff characterization.
Li and Luedtke (2023, Biometrika) and Li et al. (2023, arXiv) show modern statistical data-fusion efficiency with aligned or weakly aligned sources, distinct from sharp nonparametric bounds under bridge/drift failure.
Econometric external-validity anchors include Hotz, Imbens, and Mortimer (2005), Heckman and Vytlacil (2005), and Bisbee, Dehejia, Pop-Eleches, and Samii (2017), emphasizing heterogeneity, support, and extrapolation rather than graphical iff criteria.
Recent reviews by Degtiar and Rose (2023) and Colnet et al. (2024) show applied growth in transportability and trial/observational combination, mostly with exchangeability or weighting assumptions.
Partial-identification work from Manski to Lanners et al. (2025) and Zetterstrom et al. (2025) supplies a fallback when point identification fails, but sharp bounds for arbitrary k selected overlapping sources remain open.

banked_reason: 3 angles ACCEPT -0.5 flagship, all REJECT 0.5: derivation yielded source-cover soundness + chain-refinement counterexample, structurally a finite-chain-rule certificate inside Bareinboim-Pearl framework. Producer self-disclaimed flagship status in its own note. Reviewer correctly flagged below flagship floor.
